## CryptoUtils namespace

## Classes

<table><thead><tr><th>

Class


</th><th>

Description


</th></tr></thead>
<tbody><tr><td>

[NodeCryptoProvider](./ts-extras.cryptoutils.nodecryptoprovider.md)


</td><td>

Node.js implementation of [CryptoUtils.ICryptoProvider](./ts-extras.cryptoutils.icryptoprovider.md) using the built-in crypto module. Uses AES-256-GCM for authenticated encryption.


</td></tr>
</tbody></table>

Function


</th><th>

Description


</th></tr></thead>
<tbody><tr><td>

[createEncryptedFile(params)](./ts-extras.cryptoutils.createencryptedfile.md)


</td><td>

Creates an [encrypted file](./ts-extras.cryptoutils.iencryptedfile.md) from JSON content.


</td></tr>
<tr><td>

[decryptFile(file, key, cryptoProvider, payloadConverter)](./ts-extras.cryptoutils.decryptfile.md)


</td><td>

Decrypts an [encrypted file](./ts-extras.cryptoutils.iencryptedfile.md) and returns the JSON content.


</td></tr>
<tr><td>

[fromBase64(base64)](./ts-extras.cryptoutils.frombase64.md)


</td><td>

Decodes a base64 string to a `Uint8Array`<!-- -->.


</td></tr>
<tr><td>

[isEncryptedFile(json)](./ts-extras.cryptoutils.isencryptedfile.md)


</td><td>

Checks if a JSON object appears to be an encrypted file. Uses the format field as a discriminator.


</td></tr>
<tr><td>

[toBase64(bytes)](./ts-extras.cryptoutils.tobase64.md)


</td><td>

Encodes a `Uint8Array` to a base64 string.


</td></tr>
<tr><td>

[tryDecryptFile(json, key, cryptoProvider, payloadConverter, metadataConverter)](./ts-extras.cryptoutils.trydecryptfile.md)


</td><td>

Attempts to parse and decrypt a JSON object as an [encrypted file](./ts-extras.cryptoutils.iencryptedfile.md)<!-- -->.


</td></tr>
</tbody></table>

Generic encrypted file format. This is the JSON structure stored in encrypted files.

<tr><td>

Configuration for encrypted file handling during loading.

<tr><td>

[IKeyDerivationParams](./ts-extras.cryptoutils.ikeyderivationparams.md)


</td><td>

Key derivation parameters stored in encrypted files. Allows decryption with password without needing to know the original salt/iterations.


</td></tr>
